texts.orig: PSPO 1072: Change the Max to 100 for Labs (Remedy 274357)
texts.text: PSPO 1072: Change the Max to 100 for Labs (Remedy 274357)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – In Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) Graphical User Interface (GUI) v.27, the maximum number of reports and labs was changed from 100 to 10. (This item also listed under Reports.)
texts.text: – In Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) Graphical User Interface (GUI) v.27, the maximum number of reports and labs was changed from 100 to 10. (This item also listed under Reports.)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In CPRS v.26, if you asked to view microbiology results on the Labs tab, you viewed ALL results. Now with v.27, the default is 7 days and if you select All results, you see the last 10 reports. The same applies to the reports tab. Those settings can be changed at the Individual Reports level, which with v.27 is now set to a max of 10, whereas with v.26 that max setting was 100. The reports now look the same in both the Labs tab and the Reports tab.
texts.text: In CPRS v.26, if you asked to view microbiology results on the Labs tab, you viewed ALL results. Now with v.27, the default is 7 days and if you select All results, you see the last 10 reports. The same applies to the reports tab. Those settings can be changed at the Individual Reports level, which with v.27 is now set to a max of 10, whereas with v.26 that max setting was 100. The reports now look the same in both the Labs tab and the Reports tab.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers changed the maximum back to 100 for both Labs and Reports tabs.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers changed the maximum back to 100 for both Labs and Reports tabs.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 1082: IV Orders Were Coming across Differently in Pharmacy Package
texts.text: PSPO 1082: IV Orders Were Coming across Differently in Pharmacy Package

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, when a user entered “IN” in Infusion Order dialog’s Type filed, CPRS correctly displayed “Intermittent”, but when a pharmacy user finished the order, the Intravenous (IV) order defaulted to “Admixture”, which was incorrect.
texts.text: – Previously, when a user entered “IN” in Infusion Order dialog’s Type filed, CPRS correctly displayed “Intermittent”, but when a pharmacy user finished the order, the Intravenous (IV) order defaulted to “Admixture”, which was incorrect.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers corrected the problem. Now, when a user enters the “IN”, it correctly defaults in the dialog, and when finished in Pharmacy, the IV is a “Piggyback”, which is correct.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers corrected the problem. Now, when a user enters the “IN”, it correctly defaults in the dialog, and when finished in Pharmacy, the IV is a “Piggyback”, which is correct.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 1097: Remove CR from “Reason for Study” Field (Remedy 276791, 277509, 277788, 278589, 296988)
texts.text: PSPO 1097: Remove CR from “Reason for Study” Field (Remedy 276791, 277509, 277788, 278589, 296988)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – CPRS did not strip out carriage returns from the text included in the new “Reason for Study” field. This generally occurs when users copy text and paste it into the “Reason for Study” field. The consequence is that CPRS passes the data to the Radiology application and Radiology passes the data to PowerScribe with a carriage return, causing PowerScribe to experience an order processing error.
texts.text: – CPRS did not strip out carriage returns from the text included in the new “Reason for Study” field. This generally occurs when users copy text and paste it into the “Reason for Study” field. The consequence is that CPRS passes the data to the Radiology application and Radiology passes the data to PowerScribe with a carriage return, causing PowerScribe to experience an order processing error.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: This problem will be resolved in two steps:
texts.text: Resolution: This problem will be resolved in two steps:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 1) OR*3.0*303 and RA*5.0*98 will be released as an interim fix. These patches will detect the problem and correct the data before storing it.
texts.text: 1) OR*3.0*303 and RA*5.0*98 will be released as an interim fix. These patches will detect the problem and correct the data before storing it.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 2) OR*3*296 (CPRS GUI v27.90) will correct the source of the problem by removing any carriage returns from the text entered through the CPRS GUI. The CPRS GUI modification will resolve the problem before the data is stored or passed.
texts.text: 2) OR*3*296 (CPRS GUI v27.90) will correct the source of the problem by removing any carriage returns from the text entered through the CPRS GUI. The CPRS GUI modification will resolve the problem before the data is stored or passed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 1209: EDIT/RESUBMIT “New Comments” Stored as One Long String in the GMR(123 Node
texts.text: PSPO 1209: EDIT/RESUBMIT “New Comments” Stored as One Long String in the GMR(123 Node

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, when a user cancelled/denied a consult and added a comment using the Edit/Resubmit button, the comment was stored as a single long comment. Because it was a single long comment, the text displayed inappropriately as a long line that went off the dialog without wrapping. The way it was stored also caused problems with Health Level 7 (HL7) transmissions.
texts.text: – Previously, when a user cancelled/denied a consult and added a comment using the Edit/Resubmit button, the comment was stored as a single long comment. Because it was a single long comment, the text displayed inappropriately as a long line that went off the dialog without wrapping. The way it was stored also caused problems with Health Level 7 (HL7) transmissions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers changed the way the comments were stored. The comment should now wrap appropriately in the display and should not cause problems with HL7 transmissions for Inter-Facility Consults.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers changed the way the comments were stored. The comment should now wrap appropriately in the display and should not cause problems with HL7 transmissions for Inter-Facility Consults.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 1321: Graphing: Sometimes Lab Test Being Graphed May Not Display Its Most Recent Value (Remedy 288907)
texts.text: PSPO 1321: Graphing: Sometimes Lab Test Being Graphed May Not Display Its Most Recent Value (Remedy 288907)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously when graphing lab data for a patient and there was data caching, one of the lab tests would not display its most recent value. Data caching occurs only when the user has previously used graphing on a specific patient. Which lab test had its value dropped depended on what collection of lab tests the patient has (based on an internal number for the test). This makes the problem appear sporadic.
texts.text: – Previously when graphing lab data for a patient and there was data caching, one of the lab tests would not display its most recent value. Data caching occurs only when the user has previously used graphing on a specific patient. Which lab test had its value dropped depended on what collection of lab tests the patient has (based on an internal number for the test). This makes the problem appear sporadic.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers corrected this problem in CPRS. All values should now appear.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers corrected this problem in CPRS. All values should now appear.

texts.orig: PSPO 1349: Provider Orders BID Schedule but Gets a Different Admin Time Stored with the Order (Remedy 294361)
texts.text: PSPO 1349: Provider Orders BID Schedule but Gets a Different Admin Time Stored with the Order (Remedy 294361)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – If a user typed in a non-unique schedule and pressed the <Enter> key, CPRS allowed the order to be accepted with a non-unique schedule and the wrong administration time.
texts.text: – If a user typed in a non-unique schedule and pressed the <Enter> key, CPRS allowed the order to be accepted with a non-unique schedule and the wrong administration time.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers made several changes to resolve these issues.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers made several changes to resolve these issues.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Developers changed CPRS to require a unique schedule when leaving the schedule field in the Unit Dose dialog.
texts.text: Developers changed CPRS to require a unique schedule when leaving the schedule field in the Unit Dose dialog.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Developers changes what happens when the user presses the <Enter> key while the focus is still on a drop-down list. Previously, pressing the <Enter> key selected the item and accepted the order, but now pressing the <Enter> key will be the same as pressing the <Tab> key—it will select the item and move to the next field.
texts.text: Developers changes what happens when the user presses the <Enter> key while the focus is still on a drop-down list. Previously, pressing the <Enter> key selected the item and accepted the order, but now pressing the <Enter> key will be the same as pressing the <Tab> key—it will select the item and move to the next field.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS will also check for a control character in the schedule field when placing an order. If CPRS finds a control character, then it displays an error message to the user and the order cannot be saved until the schedule field is corrected.
texts.text: CPRS will also check for a control character in the schedule field when placing an order. If CPRS finds a control character, then it displays an error message to the user and the order cannot be saved until the schedule field is corrected.

texts.orig: Remedy 337505: NCHC--Consult “Reason for Request” Scrollbar Limitation
texts.text: Remedy 337505: NCHC--Consult “Reason for Request” Scrollbar Limitation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Northern California Health Care System reported a problem with the Consult/Procedure dialog, specifically the reason for request field. The reported issue was the scrollbar did not expand properly when text or number of lines filled the reason for request field, resulting in no visibility of text being typed in text field.  This limitation does not exist during the display of the Reason for Request field.
texts.text: – Northern California Health Care System reported a problem with the Consult/Procedure dialog, specifically the reason for request field. The reported issue was the scrollbar did not expand properly when text or number of lines filled the reason for request field, resulting in no visibility of text being typed in text field.  This limitation does not exist during the display of the Reason for Request field.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Workaround:
texts.text: Workaround:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Option #1; When user comes to the end of the reason for request field and scroll bar does not expand, user can hit enter 2-3 times and scroll bar will be enabled and text will be visible.
texts.text: Option #1; When user comes to the end of the reason for request field and scroll bar does not expand, user can hit enter 2-3 times and scroll bar will be enabled and text will be visible.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Option #2: For long reason for requests, users may use a word processing tool to write reason for request.  Then, copy and paste test in to the reason for request field.
texts.text: Option #2: For long reason for requests, users may use a word processing tool to write reason for request.  Then, copy and paste test in to the reason for request field.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers will correct this issue in CPRS GUI v.28.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers will correct this issue in CPRS GUI v.28.

texts.orig: Remedy 345650: Personal Quick Orders Don't Save All Values Entered for Some Meds
texts.text: Remedy 345650: Personal Quick Orders Don't Save All Values Entered for Some Meds

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Providers noticed that their personal quick orders did not retain the saved Quantity value. Instead, the personal quick order defaulted to a Quantity of zero. This issue only occurs with outpatient medications saved as personal quick orders involving medications where the Quantity cannot be calculated (i.e., supply orders, inhalants).
texts.text: – Providers noticed that their personal quick orders did not retain the saved Quantity value. Instead, the personal quick order defaulted to a Quantity of zero. This issue only occurs with outpatient medications saved as personal quick orders involving medications where the Quantity cannot be calculated (i.e., supply orders, inhalants).

texts.text: Workaround:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, if a user who did not have the PROVIDER or ORES key, attempted to add orders to a group of existing orders, CPRS did not prompt the user to define the encounter provider. When the user tried to accept the orders, CPRS displayed the error Unable to Save Order error and did not accept the order.
texts.text: – Previously, if a user who did not have the PROVIDER or ORES key, attempted to add orders to a group of existing orders, CPRS did not prompt the user to define the encounter provider. When the user tried to accept the orders, CPRS displayed the error Unable to Save Order error and did not accept the order.

texts.orig: CPRS Does Not Allow First Action on Change Orders, Copy Orders, or Quick Orders (Remedy 278833)
texts.text: CPRS Does Not Allow First Action on Change Orders, Copy Orders, or Quick Orders (Remedy 278833)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – In CPRS v.27, when the user selected any action that loaded previously defined order data, such as quick orders, transfer orders, change orders, or copy orders, CPRS did not correctly adjust the Quantity field in outpatient order dialog for the first change that a user would make. For example, if a user selected an order and selected the Change action, and then subsequently changed the free-text schedule to a defined schedule (such as BID), the quantity field would recalculate. But if the user then changed back to the free-text schedule, the quantity field would keep the same quantity as when the BID schedule was there. Another example would be if the user selected Change order and then changed the Days Supply value, the quantity field would not adjust.
texts.text: – In CPRS v.27, when the user selected any action that loaded previously defined order data, such as quick orders, transfer orders, change orders, or copy orders, CPRS did not correctly adjust the Quantity field in outpatient order dialog for the first change that a user would make. For example, if a user selected an order and selected the Change action, and then subsequently changed the free-text schedule to a defined schedule (such as BID), the quantity field would recalculate. But if the user then changed back to the free-text schedule, the quantity field would keep the same quantity as when the BID schedule was there. Another example would be if the user selected Change order and then changed the Days Supply value, the quantity field would not adjust.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS to recognize the first action correctly so that the quantity field will calculate, as it should. In the above scenario, CPRS will readjust the quantity to zero if the user has not done a manual correction to the quantity value. If the user does manually change the Quantity value and then makes any adjustment to the schedule, the Days Supply value should update and not the quantity value. This returns to the functionality that existed in CPRS GUI v.26.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS to recognize the first action correctly so that the quantity field will calculate, as it should. In the above scenario, CPRS will readjust the quantity to zero if the user has not done a manual correction to the quantity value. If the user does manually change the Quantity value and then makes any adjustment to the schedule, the Days Supply value should update and not the quantity value. This returns to the functionality that existed in CPRS GUI v.26.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Sites discovered a problem with the Reminders evaluation that conflicted with use of the Vitals dynamic link library (DLL) in CPRS. When a user opens a patient record in CPRS, several processes occur, including a Reminders evaluation to see if any Reminders are due for the selected patient. While this is happening, the Reminders icon shows the magnifying glass going in circles.
texts.text: – Sites discovered a problem with the Reminders evaluation that conflicted with use of the Vitals dynamic link library (DLL) in CPRS. When a user opens a patient record in CPRS, several processes occur, including a Reminders evaluation to see if any Reminders are due for the selected patient. While this is happening, the Reminders icon shows the magnifying glass going in circles.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The conflict occurred when the user opened the Vitals DLL while the Reminders evaluation was occurring. When the Vitals DLL is launched, CPRS gave control over to the DLL. When the DLL closed, it gave control back to CPRS, but the Reminders evaluation did not complete, so no Reminders were shown as due.
texts.text: The conflict occurred when the user opened the Vitals DLL while the Reminders evaluation was occurring. When the Vitals DLL is launched, CPRS gave control over to the DLL. When the DLL closed, it gave control back to CPRS, but the Reminders evaluation did not complete, so no Reminders were shown as due.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS to begin the Reminders evaluation again if it is not complete.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS to begin the Reminders evaluation again if it is not complete.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Number Templates Not Working Properly (Remedy 276520, 276117, 276757)
texts.text: Number Templates Not Working Properly (Remedy 276520, 276117, 276757)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – When CPRS v.27 was created, something changed so that sometimes number fields in templates were not working correctly. In some cases, the number field worked like a free-text field with a default value, but users could not change the value. In other cases, if the field had a “spinner” (an up and down arrow next to the field), the arrows became hidden and therefore unreachable with the mouse, but the Up and Down arrow keys on the keyboard did work.
texts.text: – When CPRS v.27 was created, something changed so that sometimes number fields in templates were not working correctly. In some cases, the number field worked like a free-text field with a default value, but users could not change the value. In other cases, if the field had a “spinner” (an up and down arrow next to the field), the arrows became hidden and therefore unreachable with the mouse, but the Up and Down arrow keys on the keyboard did work.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers corrected this problem.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers corrected this problem.

texts.orig: “Lab Results Available” Label Sometimes Misleading
texts.text: “Lab Results Available” Label Sometimes Misleading

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – In the Blood Components and Diagnostic Test Orders dialog, one of the tabs is labeled “Lab Results”. However, when data is available the tab name changes to “Lab Results Available”. The label on the tab was displaying incorrectly at times when there was no data.
texts.text: – In the Blood Components and Diagnostic Test Orders dialog, one of the tabs is labeled “Lab Results”. However, when data is available the tab name changes to “Lab Results Available”. The label on the tab was displaying incorrectly at times when there was no data.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers corrected this problem and the tab should be labeled “Lab Results Available” only when there is some data.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers corrected this problem and the tab should be labeled “Lab Results Available” only when there is some data.

texts.text: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS to correctly retrieve and send the Urgency. This change requires both OR*3.0*296 and OR*3.0*212, which activates the VBECS dialog in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Warning for Duplicate Type and Screen with Quick Orders
texts.text: Warning for Duplicate Type and Screen with Quick Orders

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – When a patient already had a Type and Screen done within the allowed period for the site (the default is 3 days), and a user placed a quick order for a Type and Screen, the user was not informed that the patient already had a valid Type and Screen.
texts.text: – When a patient already had a Type and Screen done within the allowed period for the site (the default is 3 days), and a user placed a quick order for a Type and Screen, the user was not informed that the patient already had a valid Type and Screen.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS so that if the patient has a Type and Screen done within a specified number of days, the user will get a warning that the patient has a valid Type and Screen. The user can still order a type and screen. Your clinical coordinator can define the number of days for a valid Type and Screen in a parameter.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS so that if the patient has a Type and Screen done within a specified number of days, the user will get a warning that the patient has a valid Type and Screen. The user can still order a type and screen. Your clinical coordinator can define the number of days for a valid Type and Screen in a parameter.

texts.text: – Previously, when a user entered the Blood Components and Diagnostic Test Orders dialog, and first ordered a test and then ordered a blood component, CPRS would display a warning that a Type and Screen was required, but it had really been ordered.

texts.text: Resolution: CPRS developers corrected this problem and the warning should display appropriately.

texts.text: Error Received when Choosing Type and Screen First on an Order

texts.text: – When trying to place a blood component order, if the user first selected a Type and Screen and then selected a blood component, CPRS displayed messages saying that the test was needed. Although the test was already selected, CPRS acted as if there was no Type and Screen ordered.

texts.text: Resolution: CPRS now accepts and correctly processes the tests and blood products regardless of which is ordered first.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VBECS Quick Order for “RBC Only” Does Not Require T&S even When There Is No Specimen in Lab
texts.text: VBECS Quick Order for “RBC Only” Does Not Require T&S even When There Is No Specimen in Lab

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, if the user placed a quick order for red blood cells and the Type and Screen test was not included in the order, CPRS did not prompt the user to order one when it should have. If the patient did not have a valid specimen in the lab, CPRS should have prompted the user that a Type and Screen was required. The order could therefore be sent to VBECS without the required test.
texts.text: – Previously, if the user placed a quick order for red blood cells and the Type and Screen test was not included in the order, CPRS did not prompt the user to order one when it should have. If the patient did not have a valid specimen in the lab, CPRS should have prompted the user that a Type and Screen was required. The order could therefore be sent to VBECS without the required test.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: CPRS should now prompt for Type and Screen test when one is required.
texts.text: Resolution: CPRS should now prompt for Type and Screen test when one is required.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VBECS Reason for Request  Should Expand Long Reason when Cursor Hovers over Selection
texts.text: VBECS Reason for Request  Should Expand Long Reason when Cursor Hovers over Selection

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Sites requested that when the cursor hovers over long reasons for request (those that expand beyond the boundaries of the visible field) in the Blood Component and Diagnostic Test Order Form that the hover hint shows the entire reason for request.
texts.text: – Sites requested that when the cursor hovers over long reasons for request (those that expand beyond the boundaries of the visible field) in the Blood Component and Diagnostic Test Order Form that the hover hint shows the entire reason for request.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS so that hovering the cursor over a long reason for request will show the entire reason.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS so that hovering the cursor over a long reason for request will show the entire reason.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VBECS: CPRS Auto-Accept Allows Blood Components to be Ordered without a T&S for a QO
texts.text: VBECS: CPRS Auto-Accept Allows Blood Components to be Ordered without a T&S for a QO

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Because CPRS VBECS quick orders could be set as auto-accept, it was possible for CPRS to bypass the checks that required a Type and Screen (T&S) to be ordered with specified blood components.
texts.text: – Because CPRS VBECS quick orders could be set as auto-accept, it was possible for CPRS to bypass the checks that required a Type and Screen (T&S) to be ordered with specified blood components.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: To prevent this problem and others from occurring, CPRS disabled auto-accept for CPRS VBECS quick orders. For CPRS VBECS quick orders, the dialog will display and users must accept the order from the dialog. This will ensure that appropriate checks take place.
texts.text: Resolution: To prevent this problem and others from occurring, CPRS disabled auto-accept for CPRS VBECS quick orders. For CPRS VBECS quick orders, the dialog will display and users must accept the order from the dialog. This will ensure that appropriate checks take place.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VBECS Quick Orders on Selection Should Open to the Order Dialog (Middle Tab)
texts.text: VBECS Quick Orders on Selection Should Open to the Order Dialog (Middle Tab)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, CPRS VBECS quick orders caused the Blood Components and Diagnostic Tests Order Form, which has three tabs, to open to the first tab, the Patient Information tab. Test sites requested that the form should instead open to the second tab, the Blood Bank Orders tab where providers would see the information from the quick order.
texts.text: – Previously, CPRS VBECS quick orders caused the Blood Components and Diagnostic Tests Order Form, which has three tabs, to open to the first tab, the Patient Information tab. Test sites requested that the form should instead open to the second tab, the Blood Bank Orders tab where providers would see the information from the quick order.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS so that when a quick order is selected, CPRS will open to the second tab, the Blood Bank Orders tab.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS so that when a quick order is selected, CPRS will open to the second tab, the Blood Bank Orders tab.

texts.orig: Vitals Lite
texts.text: Vitals Lite

texts.label: text
texts.orig: User Kicked Out of CPRS when Vitals Lite Opened (Remedy 287515, 287532, 287536, 287764, 287766, 288230)
texts.text: User Kicked Out of CPRS when Vitals Lite Opened (Remedy 287515, 287532, 287536, 287764, 287766, 288230)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, when a user had logged in to CPRS and while the Cover Sheet was loading, the user launched the Vitals Lite application and entered vitals. Then, the user closed Vitals Lite, later reopened it, and closed it again. At that point, CPRS inappropriately closed. One user even reported that when she opened the chart after it closed, CPRS displayed a different patient’s chart.
texts.text: – Previously, when a user had logged in to CPRS and while the Cover Sheet was loading, the user launched the Vitals Lite application and entered vitals. Then, the user closed Vitals Lite, later reopened it, and closed it again. At that point, CPRS inappropriately closed. One user even reported that when she opened the chart after it closed, CPRS displayed a different patient’s chart.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers found that the problem seemed to be when users double-clicked when launching Vitals Lite. Developers corrected the problem and a double-click will no longer cause this problem.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers found that the problem seemed to be when users double-clicked when launching Vitals Lite. Developers corrected the problem and a double-click will no longer cause this problem.
